headline: Proton Market Share Falls to 14% as Chinese EVs Surge
context: Proton's domestic market share fell to 14.2% in 2025 from 18.8% in 2023, as BYD, Chery, and Geely EVs captured 12% of new car sales while the national carmaker's EV strategy remained dependent on Geely technology transfer that has yet to materialise.

cards.big: Proton's 14.2% market share is lowest since 2016 — Chinese EVs selling at RM88,000 undercut Proton's own ICE models
cards.sub: 40 years and RM30 billion in cumulative government support later, Proton can't compete in its own protected market

cards.big: BYD Atto 3 outsold Proton X50 in Q4 2025 — first time a foreign brand outsold Proton in the SUV segment
cards.sub: EV imports at zero duty until 2027 create temporary price advantage that Proton's ICE-heavy lineup cannot match

cards.big: Proton employs 12,800 workers and supports 380 vendors — market share decline threatens entire automotive supply chain
cards.sub: Proton City in Tanjung Malim depends on the carmaker for 68% of local employment — company decline is community collapse

cards.big: Government's 2023 Proton-Geely partnership promised EV technology transfer by 2025 — no Proton-badged EV launched yet
cards.sub: DRB-HICOM board includes 4 political appointees — strategic decisions influenced by industrial policy goals rather than commercial viability

cards.big: After 40 years of protection, Proton still can't compete globally — the national car project has become a national car problem
Should Proton's protection be phased out over 5 years and the RM2 billion annual tax revenue foregone be invested in EV infrastructure instead?
